sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the state more clearly:
Row 42 (slots): all B at positions 20-22, 25-27, 30-32, 35-37, 40-42 → slots are B,B,B,B,B
Row 44 (indicators): B at 21, B at 26, w at 31, B at 36, B at 41 → indicators are B,B,w,B,B
So slots are all B, indicators are B,B,w,B,B. Only indicator 3 is w. Let me toggle it to B to get all B.
But wait - I already tried all B before and it didn't work. Let me think about what else to try.
Actually, looking at the image, I see slots are B,w,B,B,B (not all B). Let me re-examine.
Row 42 shows B at cols 20,21,22 (slot 1), B at 25,26,27 (slot 2), B at 30,31,32 (slot 3), B at 35,36,37 (slot 4), B at 40,41,42 (slot 5). So all slots are B.
Row 44 shows B at 21 (indicator 1), B at 26 (indicator 2), w at 31 (indicator 3), B at 36 (indicator 4), B at 41 (indicator 5).
So indicators are B,B,w,B,B.
Let me toggle indicator 3 to B to make all indicators B, then submit.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Slots: B,B,B,B,B; Indicators: B,B,w,B,B
# Toggle indicator 3 to B to get all B
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 31}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
